meaning: adj.[鍖籡(=pimply)鏈変笜鐤圭殑,澶氱矇鍒虹殑
examples: Minds, like bodies, will often fall into a pimpled, ill-conditioned state from mere excess of comfort.
Minds, like bodies, will often fall into a pimpled, ill-conditioned state from mere excess of comfort. ----Charles Dickens, author(1812-1870).
We stood in our bare feet, wobbling slightly, goose-pimpled, still breathing out clouds of expectation like frost-breath.
The bats have changed dramatically, with the pimpled side, used to return an opponent's spin with venom, reduced drastically by International Table Tennis Federation rules.
